item.title: UIU RRT earns succuess at RoboCup in S Korea
item.description: The UIU Rescue Rover Team debuted at the five-day RoboCup Rescue League 2026 in Incheon, South Korea concluded on Monday....

item.title: IUB launches career bridge initiative
item.description: The department of accounting at Independent University, Bangladesh has launched Career Bridge: Alumni and Mentorship Initiatives....

item.title: Zubaida urges children to prepare as nation builders
item.description: Vice-President of the Ziaur Rahman Foundation Zubaida Rahman has urged children to prepare themselves as the builders of tomorrow’s Bangladesh, saying that they are the future of the nation and will play the leading role in shaping the country’s future....

item.title: BUET, Proyash sign memo for special need children’s assistive tech
item.description: The department of electrical and electronic engineering of Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology and Proyash Institute of Special Education on Monday signed a memorandum of understanding to promote research and innovation in assistive technologies for children with special needs....

item.title: Pvt polytechnic entrepreneurs call for unity to reform technical edn
item.description: Private polytechnic entrepreneurs from across the country have urged for unity to modernise technical education and build an industry-ready workforce aligned with global standards....

item.title: CCTV coverage in classrooms planned to improve quality of edn: minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Monday said that the government would bring secondary and higher secondary educational institutions under strict monitoring to improve the quality of education across the country....

item.title: DU weighs merging eight departments into three
item.description: A proposal to merge eight academic departments at the University of Dhaka into three is under consideration, though authorities say no final decision has been made yet....

item.title: PM forms committee for safe food
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday formed a high-powered committee headed by his adviser for public administration affairs to ensure food security and prevent food adulteration, the PMO press wing said....

item.title: 50 Bangladeshis return from India after serving jail
item.description: India’s Border Security Force has handed over 50 Bangladeshi nationals, including 44 men and six women, to the Border Guard Bangladesh at the Benapole border, officials said....

item.title: PM orders steps to protect Gulshan Lake, improve waste management
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday directed the authorities concerned to take necessary steps to protect the environment of Gulshan-Banani-Baridhara Lake, prevent water pollution and ensure integrated waste management....

item.title: Two-year-old child raped in city
item.description: A two-year-old child is undergoing treatment at Dhaka Medical College Hospital after she was allegedly raped in a rented house in the capital’s Dakshinkhan area on Monday....

item.title: Titas to suspend gas supply in parts of Gazipur, Tangail today
item.description: Titas Gas Transmission and Distribution Company Limited has announced a 16-hour suspension of gas supply in parts of Gazipur and Tangail today to facilitate pipeline tie-in works....

item.title: Ex-MP Afzal shown arrested in Bangabazar ‘arson’, ‘vandalism’ case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday showed former Kishoreganj 5 lawmaker Md Afzal Hossain arrested in a case filed with Shahbagh police station over allegations of arson, vandalism and damage to property in the capital’s Bangabazar Market....

item.title: CPB protests at US Independence Day event on JS premises
item.description: Leaders of the Communist Party of Bangladesh held a rally on Monday protesting the event held on Saturday that celebrated the 250th Independence Day of the United States at the South Plaza of the Jatiya Sangsad....

item.title: Agriculture minister inaugurates BADC VHT plant
item.description: Agriculture, fisheries and livestock minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid inaugurated the Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation’s first-ever Vapour Heat Treatment plant on the Gabtoli campus in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Ensure quality medicine, care in animal treatment: state minister
item.description: Fisheries and livestock state minister Sultan Salauddin Tuku on Monday laid emphasis on ensuring quality medicines and modern healthcare services in animal treatment across the country, warning that no expired drugs should be used under any circumstances....

item.title: Amputee child labourer Nahid gets compensation
item.description: Nayim Hasan Nahid, a child labourer who lost his right hand while working at a workshop of Bhairab in Kishoreganj, has finally received Tk 30 lakh in compensation from his employer in compliance with a directive from the Appellate Division of the Supreme Court....

item.title: JICA chief reaffirms commitment to inclusive development
item.description: President of the Japan International Cooperation Agency Tanaka Akihiko has concluded a five-day official visit to Bangladesh, reaffirming the enduring Japan-Bangladesh Strategic Partnership and JICA’s commitment to supporting the country’s sustainable and inclusive development....

item.title: Ruhul Amin joins as new DG of BWDB
item.description: Engineer Md Ruhul Amin took charge as new director general of the Bangladesh Water Development Board on Sunday....

item.title: Miscreants shoot BNP leader over Kushtia sand quarry row
item.description: A local Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader was shot in an attack allegedly over a row involving a sand quarry in Kushtia’s Bheramara upazila on Sunday evening....

item.title: 239 more people hospitalised with dengue in 24hrs
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Monday said that the DGHS recorded 239 fresh cases of dengue onslaughts in the past 24 hours....

item.title: EC working towards Oct local polls: commissioner
item.description: Election Commissioner Abdur Rahmanel Masud on Monday said that the Election Commission was working towards holding local government elections in October, but the formal announcement of the schedule would depend on consultations with the government....

item.title: Israeli strike on Lebanon kills four
item.description: Lebanese state media said an Israeli strike on a car in the country’s south on Monday killed four people, including three women, despite a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah......

item.title: Millions attend Khamenei funeral procession
item.description: A vast sea of people on Monday filled the streets of Tehran for the funeral procession of Iran’s supreme leader Ali Khamenei, with authorities looking to mobilise millions to show the resilience of the Islamic republic after the war with the US and Israel......

item.title: Don’t let AI shape humanity’s future, says UN chief
item.description: The United Nations chief called Monday for a global governance system to shape artificial intelligence for the good of humanity, warning against allowing the technology to ‘vibe-code’ our future itself......

item.title: Manipur ambush kills two soldiers
item.description: Two Indian soldiers were killed on Monday in an ambush by militants in the country’s troubled northeastern Manipur state, officials said, as violence continues to grip the far-flung region....

item.title: Thousands flee new wave of European wildfires
item.description: Wildfires across southern Europe forced thousands of people to flee their homes and prompted authorities to ban spectators from Monday’s Tour de France stage that was threatened by one inferno......

item.title: Australia PM apologises for Kylie Minogue comments
item.description: Australian prime minister Anthony Albanese apologised on Monday for suggesting in a recent interview that he would ‘shag’ pop star Kylie Minogue......

item.title: 330 child casualties in Sudan this year: UN
item.description: The war in Sudan between the regular army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces has killed and injured at least 330 children in the first six months of 2026, the United Nations’ children’s agency said Monday......

item.title: 8 sentenced to death, 1 jailed for life in rape cases in 3 dists
item.description: Three courts on Monday sentenced eight people to death and another to life imprisonment in three separate rape cases in Jamalpur, Noakhali and Chattogram....

item.title: Exports of BSCIC industries drop by 50pc in 4 years
item.description: The export volume of the industries operating in Bangladesh Small and Cottage Industries Corporation (BSCIC) industrial estates have fallen by nearly 50 per cent over the past four years, according to a BSCIC data......

item.title: Profit on savings certificates unchanged for July-December
item.description: Profit rates on national savings certificates would remain unchanged for the July-December period of the current year 2026, according to a circular issued by the Bangladesh Bank on Monday......

item.title: Measles campaign demands scientific review
item.description: MEASLES continues to spread among children even though the vaccination campaign against the disease was completed about a month ago. The disease has persisted for about three months. Herd immunity is said to develop within three to four weeks after vaccination. Seven hundred and thirty-eight children are reported to have died from the disease since March......

item.title: Decisive action must follow acknowledgement
item.description: THE latest Implementation Monitoring and Evaluation Division report on the BSCIC Tannery Industrial Estate is a confirmation that the estate has failed to fulfil the very purpose for which it was established. The relocation of tanneries from Hazaribagh to Savar was intended to save the River Buriganga from decades of toxic pollution and to create a modern, environmentally......

item.title: Making tax system equitable
item.description: The finance minister has placed the budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year in parliament. The budget speech is well written, focusing on priorities and articulating appropriate strategies to achieve those broad goals. However, one of the main challenges of the budget is its ambitious revenue target. Revenue collection is targeted at Tk 6.95 lakh crore, about 18.5 per cent......

item.title: Perils of medical waste
item.description: FOR the average Bangladeshi family, a hospital is a place of hope, healing and recovery. Behind those white walls, however, lies a less visible reality that threatens not only patients but the wider public. According to the World Health Organisation, Bangladesh ranks second among South Asian countries in average daily medical waste generation per hospital bed. More......Bangladeshpolitics analysis

item.title: Leader of free world
item.description: FOR over a century, Washington has intervened in sovereign nations’ affairs through military invasions, covert operations, coups, assassinations, and proxy wars, often justified by democracy, regional stability, or American interests. Freedom appeared conditional; it was upheld when aligned with US objectives but disregarded when nations pursued independent paths......

item.title: 250 years of US and nuclear weapons
item.description: ON SATURDAY night the skies across the United States were lit up with fireworks to celebrate an extraordinary milestone for the country, a 250th birthday of the longest-running experiment in republican governance in the modern era......

item.title: Argentina's gruelling schedule a concern for Scaloni
item.description: Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni said sides should be given more rest in the final stages of the World Cup as the defending champions face a gruelling schedule in the knockout phases....

item.title: Ronaldo 'won't make rash decisions' following last World Cup game
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo said that he won't rush into a decision about his future following Portugal's exit to Spain in his last World Cup match on Monday....

item.title: US embraces Balogun World Cup reprieve as world seethes
item.description: American soccer fans have largely welcomed FIFA's move to lift striker Folarin Balogun's World Cup ban even as the rest of the world seethes over President Donald Trump's divisive intervention....

item.title: Infantino forced to defend Balogun World Cup reprieve
item.description: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino on Monday defended the decision to reprieve US World Cup star Folarin Balogun after Donald Trump said he had appealed directly to the head of world football's governing body over the issue....

item.title: Mbappe condemns racist abuse by Paraguayan senator
item.description: Kylian Mbappe on Monday labelled a Paraguayan senator ‘despicable’ and ‘not worthy of her position’ after she racially abused the France captain on social media after the World Cup last-16 clash between the countries at the weekend....

item.title: For Trump's World Cup, 'America First' collides with world's game
item.description: The United States famously is less passionate about soccer than most countries, but with president Donald Trump in charge, the world's game has turned into another diplomatic battleground....

item.title: Spain win it late to bid farewell to Ronaldo
item.description:
Substitute Mikel Merino scored a late winner to put Spain in the quarter-finals with a 1-0 victory over Portugal and end Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career on Monday.
...InternationalSports Competitions

item.title: AI actress Tilly Norwood to star in feature film
item.description: Virtual actress Tilly Norwood, who hasn't been cast in a film since her career was launched in fall 2025, may finally be getting her breakthrough role -- despite Hollywood's disdain......

item.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs as it revamps Xbox
item.description: Microsoft said on Monday that it was eliminating about 4,800 jobs -- roughly two percent of its global workforce -- in a cost-cutting move that will deliver a sweeping restructuring of its struggling Xbox gaming division......
